channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gamedeveloper.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gamedeveloper.com

item.title: Indika developer raises $5M in funding for next project
item.content:encoded:
Odd Meter, the studio that made the narrative-driven title Indika, has raised $5 million in funding.

Announced via press release, the funding is led by GEM Capital and Autotelic Ventures. GEM Capital has invested in studios like Mundfish (Atomic Heart), Sad Cat Studios (Replaced), and Unfrozen (Heroes of Might and Magic: Olden Era).

Odd Meter will be using the funding to develop its (as yet unannounced) new project. When asked if 11 Bit Studios will be acting as publisher again, Odd Meter game director Dmitry Svetlow said that, at this stage, the studio hasn't made a final decision regarding publishing.

"While we’re not ready to announce details about the project yet, it has already attracted interest, and we have several offers on the table," Svetlow said in a statement. "However, we are still debating whether we would like to partner with a publisher or pursue the self-publishing route, and are planning to make this decision a bit later into development."

Related:Alex Oxspring and The Stupendium found new studio Stupendium Softworks

In the announcement, Svetlow said the funding round is giving the studio a runway to grow the team and focus entirely on building its next game. "We're grateful to GEM Capital and Autotelic Ventures for their support and trust in our vision. We're applying everything we learned from Indika to a new project that pushes our narrative and artistic goals further, while scaling production to a new level."

GEM Capital managing director Roman Gurskiy said the company sees "long-term potential" in the team and this new chapter. "Indika stood out not only creatively, but also in how strongly it resonated with audiences and industry peers. With the studio's next project, the team has the opportunity to build something larger and more ambitious, while preserving the distinctive creative voice that made Indika stand out. We see strong long-term potential in the team and their next chapter.”

Indika was released in 2024 to critical acclaim, earning nominations at The Game Awards, BAFTA Games Awards, and DICE Awards. When speaking to Game Developer in 2025 about the development and ideas behind the project, Svetlov said that, for him, Indika is first and foremost a story about "humanity's eternal struggle with itself." Religion, then, served as the "perfect companion" to explore this theme.

"Because it's specifically in religious, mystical, 'objective' reality where the fight against 'evil' very quickly displaces the original aspiration toward 'good.' And yes, this topic has always been very interesting and close to me, as I spent a lot of time in church as a teenager and visited women's monasteries with my mother multiple times."

Related:Double Fine petitions to unionize

In 2024, the same year 11 Bit Studios published Indika, the company cut staff after canceling a console-focused game codenamed "Project 8." In addition, Game Developer learned that eight developers from the Frostpunk 2 creator were let go in December of 2023. Frostpunk 2 went out to accrue nearly 600,000 sales in 2025 after what 11 Bit Studios described as a "tough" year.

About the Author

Contributing Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Diego Nicolás Argüello is a freelance journalist and critic from Argentina. Video games helped him to learn English, so now he covers them for places like The New York Times, NPR, Rolling Stone, and more. He also runs Into the Spine, a site dedicated to fostering and supporting new writers, and co-hosted Turnabout Breakdown, a podcast about the Ace Attorney series. He’s most likely playing a rhythm game as you read this.



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/indika-developer-raises-5m-in-funding-for-next-project
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
